meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop guessing and start scaling. Use this intermediate checklist to integrate analytics, escape tutorial hell, and validate your SaaS growth with data.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start measuring. By integrating event-based analytics directly into your MVP, you transform vague feature ideas into data-driven revenue streams. This guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to automate your instrumentation, ensuring you capture user intent from the very first login.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in the trenches, stitching together API wrappers and fine-tuning prompts. You have a product, but you’re blind. Every time you push an update, you’re flying into a void, hoping users click the right buttons. This is the silent killer of SaaS startups: the illusion of progress without the feedback loop of hard data.

It is time to break the cycle. You don't need another tutorial on how to build a chat interface; you need to know why your users are churning before they reach the paywall. We are moving beyond surface-level metrics to weaponize your analytics, turning raw user events into actionable business intelligence.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that correlate with revenue. We will map these to your user journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS user flow and suggest 5 high-impact event triggers that correlate with user retention and monetization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map user journey events to current codebase'

steps.title: Automate Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Inject analytics tracking code into your core services without bloating the main business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable analytics middleware wrapper for my TypeScript backend that logs events to my chosen provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/services/analytics.ts --pattern 'instrument all user-facing endpoints'

steps.title: Validate Event Payloads
steps.description: Ensure your data is clean by creating a schema for your analytics events to prevent 'data rot'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Zod schemas for all user interaction events to ensure data consistency before sending to the analytics provider.

steps.title: Implement Real-Time User Sessions
steps.description: Track the full user session to understand the path leading to conversion or abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement session tracking logic that persists across the frontend and backend, capturing UTM parameters and referral sources.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'integrate session recording and UTM tracking'

steps.title: Automated Insights Loop
steps.description: Configure an automated report that surfaces the top 3 friction points in your funnel every Monday morning.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that queries my analytics API and summarizes the drop-off rates at the signup and checkout stages.

pro_tips: Never track everything; focus on 'Action Events' (things that move the needle) rather than 'Vanity Events' (page views).
Use a UUID for anonymous users that merges into a registered user ID once they sign up to prevent fragmented data.
Always wrap your analytics calls in a try-catch block to ensure that a failure in tracking never crashes your main application.
Implement server-side tracking for critical events to avoid ad-blockers and privacy extensions stripping your data.
A/B test your event naming conventions; clear, consistent naming is the difference between a dashboard that works and one that confuses.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait 48 hours for data accumulation, then perform a funnel analysis to identify the exact step where your users are dropping off.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start growing. Your data is waiting to tell you where the money is hidden. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
